book_claims.claim: Minestrone was a major part of Emma Morano's earlier diet
book_claims.mechanism: High vegetable, legume, and complex carbohydrate content; low glycemic; rich in micronutrients and fiber

supplementary_research.finding: Traditional minestrone soup provides high dietary fiber, vitamins C and K; regular legume-vegetable soup consumption associated with lower all-cause mortality in Italian cohorts.
Vegetable-legume soups reduce postprandial glycemia and insulin response compared to equivalent energy from solid foods.

culinary_pairings.tradition: Slow-cooked Italian soups for umami depth

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Mixed soluble/insoluble fiber; soluble fraction from beans feeds butyrate-producing microbiota
Bioavailability enhanced by cooking and presence of olive oil
Aqueous cooking matrix extracts water-soluble polyphenols; consume the broth to capture them

synergies.description: Olive oil enhances lycopene and fat-soluble vitamin absorption; adds oleocanthal anti-inflammatory effects
Basil and garlic in pesto add allicin and rosmarinic acid, extending the soup's anti-inflammatory profile
Bean varieties provide resistant starch and prebiotic fiber that synergize with vegetable polyphenols
